tasks.title: Student Assessment — Orchestration
tasks.description:
Orchestration and coordination engine for the Student Assessment feature. This task owns the planning, decomposition, state tracking, and sequencing of all 6 subtasks required to build the assessment system.

## ORCHESTRATION ENGINE ACTIVE:
✅ Planning complete: Design Discussion, Outline Plan, Detailed Plan with proof units (2026-05-21)
✅ Decomposed into 6 sequential subtasks with dependencies
✅ Real-time orchestration tracking in memory (task_2_orchestration.md)
✅ Recommendations and blockers monitored per subtask

## SUBTASKS (sequenced for parallel execution):
1. **Task #6:** Assessment Foundation (Phase 1) — ~1.5 days — NEXT
   - Firebase schema, QR generator, Firestore helpers
   - Blocker: None (start here)

2. **Task #7:** Chat Interface & QR System (Phase 2) — ~2 days
   - Assessment page, chat UI, QR display, recovery flow
   - Blocker: Task #6 | Can run parallel with Task #8

3. **Task #8:** Assessment AI Engine (Phase 3) — ~2.5 days
   - Conversation logic, guardrails, signal extraction
   - Blocker: Task #6 | Can run parallel with Task #7

4. **Task #9:** Taxonomy Mapper (Phase 4) — ~2 days
   - Skill taxonomy parsing, recommendation engine
   - Blocker: Task #8

5. **Task #10:** Integration & UX (Phases 5-6) — ~1.5 days
   - Homepage button, student page pathway display, QR recovery UI
   - Blocker: Tasks #6, #7, #9

6. **Task #11:** Testing & Validation (Phase 7) — ~2 days
   - E2E flow, AI quality, QR recovery, Firebase persistence
   - Blocker: All previous tasks

## EXECUTION STRATEGY:
- **Parallel path (recommended):** #6 → (#7 + #8 in parallel) → #9 → #10 → #11 = ~5-6 days
- **Sequential path:** #6 → #7 → #8 → #9 → #10 → #11 = ~9 days

## ORCHESTRATION RESPONSIBILITIES:
✓ Track state of all 6 subtasks (ready, in_progress, blocked, completed)
✓ Update task_2_orchestration.md after each subtask completes
✓ Recommend next task based on dependency graph
✓ Monitor for blockers and flag issues
✓ Optimize for parallel execution where possible
✓ Guide workflow from initial start to final testing

## NEXT ACTION:
Run `/start-build 6` to begin Task #6: Assessment Foundation

## TRACKING & STATUS:
- Orchestration file: ~/.claude/projects/C--Users-scott-Code-Aesop/memory/task_2_orchestration.md
- Backlog details: docs/backlog.json (Tasks 6-11)
- Build plan: docs/3-Build_Plan.md (full design, outline, detailed plan, proof units)
- Session notes: Aesop_Sessions/2026-05-21_task-2-planning.md

tasks.title: Task #2a: Assessment Foundation (Phase 1)
tasks.description:
Build the infrastructure foundation for student assessment. This task establishes the data schema, QR code system, and Firebase utilities needed by all downstream components.

## What's Included:
- Extend Firebase Firestore learner record schema: add assessment_results, recommended_pathway, qr_recovery_token fields with TypeScript/JS interfaces
- Build QR code generator utility (qr-generator.js): creates QR codes encoding learner ID + optional recovery token; returns SVG/canvas element
- Build Firestore query/update helpers: read/write assessment data, pathway, QR tokens with error handling and offline fallback

## Files Modified:
- ai-academy/js/firebase-schema.js (new or extend existing)
- ai-academy/js/learner.js (extend)
- ai-academy/js/qr-generator.js (new)
- ai-academy/js/firebase-helpers.js (new or extend existing)

## Effort: ~1.5 days
## Blocker: None (START HERE)
## Dependencies: None

## Success Criteria:
✅ Firestore schema updated with assessment fields
✅ QR generator creates valid QR codes
✅ Firebase helpers read/write assessment data reliably
✅ Offline fallback queues writes for later sync

tasks.title: Task #2b: Assessment Chat Interface & QR System (Phase 2)
tasks.description:
Build the student-facing assessment page and QR recovery system. This includes the chat interface where students answer assessment questions, completion screen with QR display, and recovery flows.

## What's Included:
- Assessment page HTML (assessment.html): chat message area, user input field, submit button, QR display placeholder, navigation
- Assessment CSS (assessment.css): styling matching Aesop design, responsive layout
- Chat message rendering & input handling (assessment-chat.js): render alternating student/AI messages, clear input after submission, reuse lab chat patterns
- QR code display on completion: after assessment ends, generate QR code, display with recovery instructions, show "Start Courses" or "View Dashboard" buttons
- QR recovery UI: add "Recover Learner ID" section on homepage + student page, scan QR or enter recovery token, restore learner ID to localStorage

## Files Modified:
- ai-academy/assessment.html (new)
- ai-academy/css/assessment.css (new)
- ai-academy/js/assessment-chat.js (new)
- ai-academy/index.html (add recovery UI)
- ai-academy/students.html (add recovery UI)
- ai-academy/js/qr-recovery.js (new)

## Effort: ~2 days
## Blocker: Task #6 (Assessment Foundation)
## Dependencies: Task #6; can run in parallel with Task #8 (AI Engine)

## Success Criteria:
✅ Assessment page loads and displays correctly
✅ Chat messages render and flow naturally
✅ QR code displays on completion with clear instructions
✅ QR recovery flow restores learner ID correctly

tasks.title: Task #2c: Assessment AI Engine (Phase 3)
tasks.description:
Build the AI-driven assessment conversation engine. This generates dynamic assessment questions, extracts student aptitude and interest signals, and determines conversation flow.

## What's Included:
- Assessment prompt/guardrail design (assessment-prompts.js): system prompt that asks aptitude/interest questions, dynamically determines next question, extracts structured signals (aptitude score 0-100, interest tags), includes assessment-specific guardrail (distinct from lab guardrail)
- Assessment conversation logic (assessment-chat.js + assessment-proxy.php): wire chat to proxy.php endpoint, call Anthropic API with assessment prompt + conversation history, extract structured output (next question, aptitude/interest signals, completion flag)
- Signal extraction parser (assessment-parser.js): extract aptitude score, interest tags, completion flag from AI responses for Phase 4 consumption

## Files Modified:
- ai-academy/js/assessment-prompts.js (new)
- aesop-api/assessment-guardrail.txt (new, or config file)
- aesop-api/assessment-proxy.php (new or modify proxy.php)
- aesop-api/assessment-api.js (new)
- ai-academy/js/assessment-chat.js (extend from #7)
- ai-academy/js/assessment-parser.js (new)

## Effort: ~2.5 days
## Blocker: Task #6 (Foundation); can start in parallel with Task #7 (Chat UI)
## Dependencies: Task #7 recommended (to understand chat structure), but Task #6 required

## Success Criteria:
✅ Assessment AI asks meaningful questions about aptitude/interest
✅ AI dynamically adapts based on student responses
✅ Structured signals (aptitude score, interest tags) extracted reliably
✅ Guardrail prevents off-topic responses
✅ Assessment ends gracefully with clear completion signal

tasks.title: Task #2d: Taxonomy Mapper (Phase 4)
tasks.description:
Build the course recommendation engine that maps student aptitude/interest signals to personalized learning pathways. Converts AI signals into a curated list of courses.

## What's Included:
- Load skill taxonomy from courses-v2.html (taxonomy-parser.js): parse courses-v2.html, extract skill taxonomy (COVERAGE/EVIDENCE matrices), build in-memory map of skills → courses
- Taxonomy mapper function (taxonomy-mapper.js): takes aptitude score + interest tags, queries taxonomy, recommends primary course + 2-3 follow-up courses based on skill overlap, returns structured pathway object
- Integrate mapper into assessment completion (assessment-chat.js): after AI completes and signals extracted (Phase 3), call mapper, store pathway for Phase 2.3 (QR display) and Phase 6 (persistence)

## Files Modified:
- ai-academy/js/taxonomy-parser.js (new)
- ai-academy/js/taxonomy-mapper.js (new)
- ai-academy/js/assessment-chat.js (extend)

## Effort: ~2 days
## Blocker: Task #8 (AI Engine output required)
## Dependencies: Task #8 (needs aptitude/interest signals)

## Success Criteria:
✅ Taxonomy parses from courses-v2.html correctly
✅ Mapper generates relevant course pathways
✅ Aptitude/interest signals align with recommended courses
✅ Pathway includes primary + follow-up courses with skill progression
✅ Pathway is available to Phase 6 and stored in learner record

tasks.title: Task #2e: Integration & UX (Phases 5-6)
tasks.description:
Wire assessment into the homepage and student dashboard. Makes assessment discoverable and displays personalized pathways to students after completion.

## What's Included:
- Homepage integration (Phase 5): add "Take Assessment" button/link to homepage (visible before login, alongside "Explore Courses"); route to assessment.html
- Conditional logic on homepage: check if learner has taken assessment (Firestore assessment_results exists); show "View Pathway" instead of "Take Assessment" if true; offer "Re-take Assessment" option
- Student page pathway section (Phase 6): add new "Recommended Pathway" section on students.html displaying course list from learner record
- Pathway display & navigation: render pathway courses as clickable cards (title, description, difficulty); clicking routes to course page; show "Start Next Course" CTA
- QR recovery UI on student page: add "Lost your learner ID?" section with link to QR recovery flow; warn that recovery requires saved QR code or recovery token

## Files Modified:
- ai-academy/index.html (add assessment button + conditional logic)
- ai-academy/js/homepage.js (new or extend)
- ai-academy/students.html (add pathway section + QR recovery UI)
- ai-academy/css/homepage.css (extend or new)
- ai-academy/css/students.css (extend or new)
- ai-academy/js/pathway-display.js (new)
- ai-academy/js/qr-recovery.js (extend from #7)

## Effort: ~1.5 days
## Blocker: Tasks #6, #7, #9
## Dependencies: #6 (Foundation), #7 (Chat UI), #9 (Mapper with pathway data)

## Success Criteria:
✅ "Take Assessment" button visible on homepage
✅ Homepage shows "View Pathway" if assessment already taken
✅ Student page displays recommended pathway with courses
✅ Courses are clickable and route to course pages
✅ QR recovery option is visible and functional

tasks.title: Task #2f: Testing & Validation (Phase 7)
tasks.description:
Complete end-to-end testing and validation of the assessment system. Confirms all components work together, AI quality is acceptable, and user experience is smooth.

## What's Included:
- Manual end-to-end walkthrough: new student lands on homepage → clicks "Take Assessment" → answers questions → receives pathway → pathway persists on learner record → pathway visible on student page → can click to start courses
- AI recommendation quality validation: run assessment 5-10 times with varied student profiles (high aptitude/low interest, low aptitude/high interest, etc.); verify recommendations vary appropriately, courses are relevant to signals, no hallucinations
- QR recovery testing: verify QR displays correctly on completion, QR scans/recovers correctly, recovery restores learner ID to localStorage, student can access record via recovered ID
- Firebase persistence testing: verify assessment results and pathway persist to Firestore, QR recovery tokens stored and retrievable, offline mode queues writes for sync

## Files Modified:
None (manual/observational testing)

## Effort: ~2 days
## Blocker: All previous tasks (6-10)
## Dependencies: Tasks #6, #7, #8, #9, #10 (all must be complete)

## Success Criteria:
✅ E2E flow works: assessment → pathway → display → courses
✅ AI generates appropriate recommendations (80%+ accuracy in spot-checks)
✅ QR code generates, displays, and recovers correctly
✅ Firebase persistence works online and offline
✅ No obvious bugs or UX issues
✅ Student can complete full journey without getting lost
